bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueF, which is worst for me - utility = 0.2 across all issues), giving me a utility of only 0.2. This is very early (t=0.00), so I should counter with my ideal bid to maximize utility. My top utility is achieved with valueA on all issues (utility = 1.0). I'll open strong with my best possible offer.

It's very early (t=0.02), so I can afford to stay near my top utility. The opponent started with all valueF (worst for me) and slightly moved issueA to valueE, showing minimal concession. I should counter with a strong bid but perhaps make a small concession on my least important issue (issueD) to signal willingness to negotiate. My utility for all-A is 1.0; I'll concede issueD to valueB (minor cost: 0.1 * 0.16 = 0.016 loss) to show good faith while staying very high overall.

The opponent is conceding on issueA (from valueF → valueE → valueC) but holding firm on issueB, issueC, issueD at valueF. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my top utility. I'll hold firm on issueA (valueA) and issueB (valueA) which are my highest-weight issues, and make a small concession on issueC (moving to valueB) and issueD (keeping valueB) to signal willingness to negotiate while maintaining high utility.

The opponent is conceding on issueA (F→E→C→B) but holding firm on issueB (F→F→E→E) and issueC/D (all F). Their latest offer gives me utility: 0.4*0.84 + 0.3*0.35 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.1*0.35 = 0.336+0.105+0.04+0.035 = 0.516. We're very early (t=0.06), so I should stay near my top bids but make a small concession. I'll concede slightly on issueD (lowest weight) and issueC, while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

The opponent is conceding gradually - they moved from all valueF to now offering valueC on issueA and issueB, valueE on issueC and issueD. Their latest offer gives me utility: 0.4*0.68 + 0.3*0.68 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.1*0.35 = 0.272 + 0.204 + 0.07 + 0.035 = 0.581. We're still very early (t=0.08), so I should stay near my top utility but make small concessions. I'll hold firm on issueA (highest weight) and issueB, while conceding slightly on issueC and issueD (lower weights) to signal willingness to cooperate.

The opponent is conceding gradually - they've moved from all valueF to valueB on issueA, valueD on issueB, valueE on issueC, valueD on issueD. Their latest offer gives me utility: 0.4*0.84 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.2*0.35 + 0.1*0.52 = 0.336 + 0.156 + 0.07 + 0.052 = 0.614. We're still early (t=0.10), so I should stay near my top utility but make a small concession. I'll hold firm on issueA (highest weight) and issueB, but concede slightly on issueC and issueD to show movement.

The opponent's latest offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueF) gives me utility: 0.4*1.0 + 0.3*0.68 + 0.2*0.84 + 0.1*0.20 = 0.40 + 0.204 + 0.168 + 0.02 = 0.792. That's quite good! The opponent has been conceding steadily and just offered valueA on issueA (my most important issue). I should respond with a bid that's close to their offer but improves on issueB and issueD where I care more. I'll offer issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueD — this gives me 0.4*1.0 + 0.3*0.84 + 0.2*0.84 + 0.1*0.52 = 0.40 + 0.252 + 0.168 + 0.052 = 0.872, while being a reasonable counter that concedes issueC to valueB (matching their offer) and moves issueB up slightly from their valueC.

The opponent's latest offer (t=0.14) is issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueF. My utility for this would be: 0.4*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.2*0.68 + 0.1*0.20 = 0.4 + 0.3 + 0.136 + 0.02 = 0.856. That's quite high! The opponent has conceded significantly on issueA and issueB (both now at valueA). The only weak point is issueD: valueF (lowest utility for me, but issueD has only 0.1 weight). I should accept or counter very close to this. Since I can't "accept" explicitly, I'll counter with something very close - matching their offer but improving issueD slightly. Actually, I should just mirror their offer closely since it gives me 0.856 utility. I'll offer issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueD to slightly improve issueD for me while staying very attractive to them.

The opponent has been gradually conceding - they offered issueA: valueA and issueB: valueA at t=0.14, which is very favorable to me. Their latest offer at t=0.16 stepped back slightly (issueA: valueB, issueB: valueC), but overall they're showing willingness to concede on high-weight issues. We're still early (t=0.16), so I can maintain a strong position. My last bid was {A:valueA, B:valueA, C:valueC, D:valueD} with utility ~0.4*1+0.3*1+0.2*0.68+0.1*0.52 = 0.4+0.3+0.136+0.052 = 0.888. I'll slightly concede on issueD (low weight) to show movement while holding firm on issueA and issueB.
